items.headline: Iranian delegation arrives in Islamabad, sets preconditions for peace
items.summary: Iranian delegation led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf arrives in Islamabad for US talks, demanding a Lebanon ceasefire and release of frozen assets first. US VP Vance warns Iran against playing games. An Iranian negotiating delegation, led by Parliament Speak…

items.headline: Pope Leo Denounces The 'Delusion Of Omnipotence' He Says Fuels The U.S.-Israeli War In Iran
items.summary: Pope Leo called for all people of good will to pray for peace and demand an end to war from their political leaders.

items.headline: In the Gulf, states are 'paying the price' for a war they didn't start
items.summary: When the Iran war ends, Gulf nations will not only have to repair the economic and physical damage caused by Iran's retaliatory campaign, but will also have to learn to live with a regime that analysts say is "less predictable and more violent than it was two…

items.headline: Evangelicals amplify Trump's religious framing of Iran war
items.summary: President Trump is using Christian rhetoric to rally core supporters behind the increasingly unpopular war with Iran, religious and political experts say.

items.headline: Ceasefire brings some relief for Iranians but economic outlook remains grim
items.summary: Activity picks up in Tehran's Grand Bazaar compared with before the ceasefire, but vendors say sales remain slow.

items.headline: No retreat at Hormuz — Iran must not control the world’s energy lifeline
items.summary: A comprehensive sanctions regime including China is needed to deter Iran from weaponizing the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global trade artery.

items.headline: How serious is the Iranian sea mine threat in the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: Iran claims to have littered a central shipping passage in the Strait of Hormuz with sea mines, in a move analysts say could be a "coercive threat" to maintain its grip on the crucial waterway.

items.headline: Trump criticises Iran over continued blockade of Strait of Hormuz; oil prices rise again
items.summary: Oil ‌prices climbed in early trade due to anxiety as tanker traffic through the strait remained largely frozen

items.headline: How the Iran war energy crisis is echoing across Canada
items.summary: The global energy crisis sparked by the Iran war is shaking up Canada coast to coast and could have a lasting legacy. Read more

items.headline: Ambition will not counteract ambition
items.summary: I have no objection per se to invoking impeachment and the 25th Amendment in the context of Trump’s genocidal threats — telling the truth can be its own justification even without an immediate practical remedy, and the political context might change down the …

items.headline: JD Vance Proves Irony Is Dead as He Calls Out “Preposterous” Behavior

items.headline: Elizabeth Economy: Amanpour And Company
items.summary: Hoover Institution fellow Elizabeth Economy discusses Beijing’s strategy to expand its power in the new world order.

items.headline: Sign Of The Times
items.summary: If you haven’t seen the movie, Project Hail Mary, then you should… it is excellent. It is faithful to Andy Weir’s 2021 book, hilarious and heart-warming, and with the Artemis II crew circling the moon, the film is just what we needed.

items.headline: Markets’ dilemma: Trust the bark or wag of oil prices
items.summary: Split signals. Surge in physical oil prices alongside a steep fall in futures underscores a stark divergence, with the gap between the two widening to historic highs

items.headline: Dow Jones Surges Past 48,000 as Iran Ceasefire Sparks Massive Wall Street Rally
items.summary: NEW YORK — The Dow Jones Industrial Average blasted through the 48,000 milestone Friday morning, climbing more than 280 points in early trading on April 10, 2026, as investors cheered the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire and anticipated lower energy prices and red…

items.headline: Standard Chartered: Oil Price Correction Is Likely Overdone
items.summary: Oil prices have declined by the biggest margin since the Iran war began in late February, with Brent crude for June delivery and WTI for May delivery retreating to the mid-$90s per barrel, alongside falling refined product prices. The United States and Iran a…

items.headline: Iran Conflict Disrupts Critical Helium, Aluminum Supplies with Global Economic Impact
items.summary: A military conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran is causing severe disruptions to global supplies of helium and aluminum, threatening semiconductor manufacturing, medical equipment production, and a wide range of consumer goods, according to in…

items.headline: How Pakistan managed to get the US and Iran to a ceasefire
items.summary: Backchannel talks led by Pakistan result in temporary ceasefire after weeks of fighting.

items.summary: The Saudi energy ministry confirmed on Thursday that Iranian strikes on the kingdom's East-West pipeline have reduced its export capacity by approximately 700,000 barrels per day (bpd). The ministry stated the attacks hit a pumping station on the vital pipeli…

items.headline: Russia Expands Gasoline Export Ban, Faces Major Oil Outages Due to Drone Strikes
items.summary: The Russian government has expanded a ban on gasoline exports to include producers of the fuel, according to a report from Interfax news agency [1]. The ban, which now includes all gasoline producers, is scheduled to remain in effect until July 31. It expands…
